spotify_playlists.description: Feel-good pop anthems to match your bright mood

spotify_playlists.name: Mood Booster
spotify_playlists.description: Uplifting tracks across genres — pure positive energy

food.why: Bright colors signal nutritional variety; eating joyfully enhances flavor perception through reward circuits.
Triggers endorphin and serotonin release; flavonoids improve cerebral blood flow (Salimpoor et al., 2011).
Folate in avocado supports dopamine synthesis; healthy fats improve brain membrane function.
Citrus scent and vitamin C are independently linked to elevated mood and reduced cortisol.

places.description: Lively outdoor spaces and social spots to amplify your joy

activities.why: Movement + music amplifies positive emotion through dopamine and endorphin release simultaneously.

activities.item: Share good news with someone or call them
activities.why: Sharing positive emotions (capitalisation) doubles their intensity and duration (Langston, 1994).
Flow states are most accessible when baseline mood is positive — best time to start something new.

activities.item: Write 3 things in a gratitude journal
activities.why: Gratitude journaling extends positive emotional states and builds resilience (Emmons & McCullough, 2003).

colors.name: Sunshine Yellow
colors.why: Yellow has the highest luminosity visible to humans, triggering serotonin and activating left prefrontal cortex (positive affect hemisphere).

colors.name: Coral Orange
colors.why: Combines red's energy with yellow's warmth. Shown to increase perceived approachability by 23% in social settings (Hemphill, 1996).

colors.name: Turquoise
colors.why: Combines calming blue with energizing yellow. Lowers blood pressure while maintaining positive arousal — ideal for sustained happiness.

shows_movies.title: La La Land (2016)
shows_movies.why: Musical celebration of dreams and joy; vibrant colors and music amplify existing positive state.

shows_movies.title: Amélie (2001)
shows_movies.why: Whimsical French delight about creating happiness for others — mirrors joy's prosocial quality.

shows_movies.title: Ted Lasso
shows_movies.why: Relentlessly optimistic coaching comedy; the warmest, most joy-affirming show on television.

shows_movies.title: The Great British Bake Off
shows_movies.why: Warm, competitive, and wholesome — the emotional antithesis of toxic competition.

social.item: Call the friends who make you happy
social.why: Happiness is amplified by emotional resonance — share with those who respond with active, constructive engagement.

social.item: Reach out to someone you haven't spoken to in a while
social.why: Positive states reduce social anxiety; happy days are ideal for relationship maintenance and reconnection.

social.item: Surprise-visit a friend
social.why: Group activities during positive states create shared memories that strengthen social bonds long-term.

personal_development.why: Happy states expand cognitive breadth and are optimal for innovation and creative thinking (Ashby et al., 1999).
Broaden-and-build cycle: positive emotions literally expand the vision of what's possible (Fredrickson, 2001).
Expressing gratitude during positive states creates lasting relational bonds and deepens meaning.

spotify_playlists.name: Summer Hits
spotify_playlists.description: High-energy seasonal anthems for peak joy

food.item: Pizza or favorite meal with friends
food.why: Celebratory eating with preferred foods reinforces the positive state and creates memory anchors.
Cold + sweet triggers rapid dopamine response; treat foods are amplified by joy state.
Aesthetic food preparation increases mindful savoring; anthocyanins support continued dopaminergic activity.

places.description: Social venues and exciting spaces to celebrate your happiness

activities.why: Moderate joy is the optimal state for social bonding — connections made now are deeper and more lasting.
Anticipation of positive events extends happiness before they even occur (Gilbert, 2006).

activities.item: Go to a concert or event
activities.why: Shared positive experiences in crowds create collective joy amplification through emotional contagion.

colors.name: Lime Green
colors.why: Nature green activates biophilic responses and promotes feelings of vitality and growth.

colors.name: Hot Pink
colors.why: High saturation pink energizes and signals playfulness — perfect amplifier for moderate joy.

colors.name: Electric Blue
colors.why: Energizing yet stable — prevents joy from tipping into anxiety while amplifying positive arousal.

shows_movies.why: Inspiring adventure of self-discovery — perfect for riding moderate joy into motivation.

shows_movies.title: Parks and Recreation
shows_movies.why: Feel-good comedy about passionate civic service; love for community mirrors moderate joy's social quality.

social.item: Organize a large gathering
social.why: Group celebrations during moderate joy create collective memory anchors.

social.item: Share something positive on social media
social.why: Sharing uplifts others and extends your own positive state through reciprocal validation.

personal_development.item: Enroll in or start a challenging goal
personal_development.why: Moderate positive emotions literally expand cognitive scope — you see more possibilities and are more resilient to challenge.

spotify_playlists.name: Pop Rising
spotify_playlists.description: Biggest hits riding the wave of pure elation

food.why: Milestone meals create lasting episodic memories that anchor positive identity for years.
Creating something celebratory extends engagement with the exceptional state.

places.description: Landmark experiences to match your peak joy

activities.why: Collective joy amplifies individual joy exponentially — peak states deserve peak sharing.

activities.item: Start something you have long dreamed of
activities.why: Peak positive states provide the activation energy for long-deferred dreams (Isen, 2001).

colors.name: Gold
colors.why: Gold symbolizes preciousness across all human cultures — activates implicit associations of being valued and celebrated.

colors.name: Crimson
colors.why: High-arousal red amplifies peak states; culturally universal signal of celebration and passion.

shows_movies.title: Singin' in the Rain (1952)
shows_movies.why: Timeless peak musical joy — designed to produce the most infectious positive affect in cinema.

shows_movies.title: Schitt's Creek
shows_movies.why: Multiple beautiful love stories and community joy — mirrors intense happiness's social and connective quality.

social.item: Reach out to the most important people in your life
social.why: Peak joy shared with those who matter most creates the strongest relational memories.

personal_development.item: Write down your biggest life goal and start it
personal_development.why: Peak joy creates the neurochemical conditions for maximum motivation and risk tolerance.

spotify_playlists.name: Sad Songs
spotify_playlists.description: Emotional ballads that hold space for your feelings

spotify_playlists.name: Life Sucks
spotify_playlists.description: Raw, honest music for when things feel heavy

food.why: Apigenin binds GABA receptors, producing anxiolytic effect. Warmth activates social safety circuits (IJzerman & Semin, 2009).
Complex carbs stabilize blood sugar; banana provides tryptophan — a direct serotonin precursor, clinically validated for mild low mood.
Serotonin and endorphin release; small quantities effectively shift reward circuitry from loss to pleasure.

places.description: Calming, restorative spaces where you can breathe and reflect

activities.why: Sunlight boosts serotonin production and regulates circadian rhythm — proven antidepressant effect.

activities.item: Write about your feelings for 10 minutes
activities.why: Expressive writing reduces amygdala reactivity; Pennebaker (1997) showed lasting mood improvement after just 3 sessions.
Activates parasympathetic nervous system; increases GABA — mild sadness responds strongly within single session.

colors.name: Soft Coral Pink
colors.why: Baker-Miller Pink has documented calming effects. Soft coral provides warmth without overstimulation — most dignifying shade for sadness.

colors.name: Sage Green
colors.why: Nature's most healing color. Green is processed without chromatic aberration, reducing eye strain — the physiological burden of sadness.

colors.name: Warm Terracotta
colors.why: Earthy, grounded tones activate a sense of primal safety and belonging — evolutionary connotations of shelter and home.

shows_movies.title: Inside Out (2015)
shows_movies.why: Validates the necessity and value of sadness itself — the only film designed to make you comfortable with this emotion.

shows_movies.title: About Time (2013)
shows_movies.why: Bittersweet meditation on presence and love — teaches savoring without requiring forced positivity.

shows_movies.title: After Life (Ricky Gervais)
shows_movies.why: Honest depiction of grief with moments of warmth; validates feeling without wallowing.

social.item: Message a trusted friend
social.why: Social connection is the single strongest predictor of emotional recovery — even a brief message helps.

social.item: Be quietly around others rather than alone
social.why: Social presence without social demand allows emotional processing without isolation.

personal_development.why: Sad states provide access to psychological material defended against in ordinary states — rich time for self-knowledge.
Neff (2011): treating yourself as you'd treat a good friend in pain is more effective than self-esteem for navigating sadness.

spotify_playlists.name: Sad Indie
spotify_playlists.description: Reflective indie tracks that sit with you in the grey

spotify_playlists.name: Dark & Stormy
spotify_playlists.description: Deep introspection music for heavier moments

food.why: EPA/DHA omega-3s: meta-analysis of 13 RCTs shows antidepressant-comparable effects (Grosso et al., 2014).
Warm liquid raises core temperature, mimicking social warmth's effect on belonging circuits.
Folate, iron, protein — nutritional deficiencies significantly worsen moderate depressive symptoms.

places.description: Nature and peaceful environments proven to reduce rumination

activities.why: Aerobic exercise increases BDNF — functions like natural antidepressant. Single walk shifts neurochemistry.
Combined thermotherapy + aromatherapy; lavender in RCTs reduces depressive symptoms within 20 minutes.

activities.item: Have a voice call with someone you trust
activities.why: Verbal emotional expression reduces limbic activation more than written expression (James-Lange theory application).

colors.name: Dusty Blue
colors.why: Honors the emotional state while dignifying it. Dusty blue says 'this is real' without deepening depression.

colors.name: Warm Peach
colors.why: Skin-adjacent tones activate social safety perceptions at a pre-cognitive level — deeply reassuring to the saddened nervous system.

shows_movies.title: The Shawshank Redemption (1994)
shows_movies.why: Hope against despair — emotionally cathartic without bypassing sadness; models resilience without toxic positivity.

shows_movies.title: Normal People
shows_movies.why: Intense but beautiful exploration of love and sadness — validates complexity without resolution.

social.item: Talk to someone who has experienced similar pain
social.why: Shared experience of sadness creates the deepest social communion — reduces isolating feeling that 'no one understands.'

social.item: Go to someone who loves you
social.why: Receiving care during sadness activates the attachment system — most direct neurological path from aloneness to belonging.

personal_development.why: CBT and IPT have the highest evidence base for moderate depressive states — professional support is advanced self-care.

personal_development.item: Express yourself through art or music
personal_development.why: Art therapy meta-analyses show significant reduction in depression scores. Non-verbal expression accesses unreachable emotional material.

spotify_playlists.name: Peaceful Piano
spotify_playlists.description: Gentle piano to hold the weight of difficult emotions

spotify_playlists.name: Classical for Relaxation
spotify_playlists.description: Orchestral comfort for when words are not enough

food.why: Tryptophan in milk; honey glucose aids brain transport. Traditional comfort food has biochemical basis.
Tryptophan + healthy fat combination; eating is itself a self-care act when deeply sad.

places.description: Safe, comforting spaces for rest and gentle recovery

activities.item: Let yourself cry — do not suppress it
activities.why: Emotional tears contain stress hormones including cortisol; crying is a physiological self-regulation mechanism.
Deep pressure stimulation reduces cortisol and increases serotonin and dopamine — clinically studied intervention.
Intense or prolonged sadness beyond 2 weeks affecting daily function warrants professional support — not weakness.

colors.name: Ivory White
colors.why: Maximum luminosity, minimum threat. Creates 'clean slate' cognitions that counter hopelessness patterns.

colors.name: Lavender
colors.why: Lavender's unique dual properties slightly energize while calming — prevents the flatness of purely cool colors.

shows_movies.title: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind (2004)
shows_movies.why: Profound exploration of loss and memory — provides companionship in the deepest grief.

shows_movies.title: Six Feet Under
shows_movies.why: Award-winning meditation on grief and meaning — the most honest portrayal of intense sadness on television.

social.item: Call your closest trusted person — now
social.why: Co-regulation: nervous system calms faster in presence of a regulated, caring other.

personal_development.why: Micro-accomplishments restore tiny amounts of self-efficacy which compound meaningfully over time.

spotify_playlists.name: Metal Classics

food.why: Hydration reduces physical arousal; cold temperature activates dive reflex, slowing heart rate within seconds.
Crunching provides physical outlet for jaw tension; magnesium in vegetables reduces arousal response.
L-theanine promotes alpha brainwave activity — calm alertness without the sedation that prevents functional resolution.

places.description: Open, energetic spaces to move and metabolize anger

activities.why: Physical movement metabolizes adrenaline and cortisol that sustain anger. 90-second rule: neurochemical anger lasts only 90 seconds without rumination.
Extended exhale activates vagus nerve; parasympathetic activation interrupts anger within 90 seconds.

activities.item: Submerge your face in cold water
activities.why: Mammalian dive reflex: cold water on face reduces heart rate up to 25% within seconds — fastest physiological intervention.

colors.name: Calm Navy Blue
colors.why: Blue is the most physiologically calming color. Navy rooms shown to reduce heart rate and cortisol within minutes of exposure.

colors.name: Forest Green
colors.why: Deep natural green activates parasympathetic system. In studies, green environments reduced aggressive behavior by 30%.

colors.name: Stone Gray
colors.why: Neutral gray creates psychological distance from anger's heat — ego-stabilizing and proprioceptively calming.

shows_movies.title: The Dark Knight (2008)
shows_movies.why: Channels moral rage into principled action — models transforming anger into meaningful force.

shows_movies.title: Breaking Bad
shows_movies.why: Study of what happens when anger and pride go unchecked — cathartically warns against the very state you're in.

social.item: Do NOT contact the person you are angry at yet
social.why: Hot cognition impairs perspective-taking. The 90-second rule: wait until physiological anger has fully subsided.

social.item: Call a calm friend — just to be heard
social.why: Seek presence, not vengeance-validation. Friends who agree amplify rather than regulate anger.

personal_development.item: Write down which of your values or boundaries was violated
personal_development.why: Anger is always a signal of a violated value or need. Identifying it converts reactive emotion to actionable information.
Chronic anger often accumulates in people who have difficulty expressing boundaries — assertiveness is the preventive skill.

Phenylethylamine and endorphins counter anger's stress chemistry.
Complex carbs support serotonin synthesis which counteracts anger's dopaminergic dominance.

places.description: High-energy spaces to safely metabolize intense anger

activities.why: Most reliable anger regulator: aerobic exercise metabolizes cortisol and adrenaline completely.

activities.item: Write it all out (do not send!)

colors.name: Cool Lavender

colors.name: Teal

shows_movies.title: The Wire

social.item: Go for sports or a walk — with a friend
social.why: Physical co-regulation with a friend provides metabolic catharsis plus social support — dual regulation mechanism.

personal_development.why: Rosenberg (2003): NVC converts anger into productive boundary expression — the language of needs rather than accusations.

places.description: Isolation and physical release spaces for peak anger

activities.item: Stay alone in a safe place
activities.why: Removing social context reduces risk of relationship damage during peak anger state.

social.item: Be physically present with the person who calms you

personal_development.item: Build a fear hierarchy and approach it step by step

activities.item: Immediately follow the curiosity that surprise brings

activities.item: Walk somewhere you have never been before
activities.why: Embodied novelty-seeking creates positive feedback loop of curiosity and discovery (Berlyne, 1971).

social.why: Surprise shared is surprise doubled. Social transmission of surprising information is how communities update world models.

social.item: Talk to someone from a different culture or background
social.why: Surprise opens mind to genuine otherness — cross-cultural conversations during surprise produce the most fruitful exchanges.

personal_development.item: Reflect and write about why you did not expect this
personal_development.why: Metafeedback on your own surprise calibration builds epistemic awareness — improves future pattern recognition.

personal_development.item: Research something you have long been curious about
personal_development.why: Surprise neuroplasticity window: surprised brain is maximally open to new information and long-term retention.

activities.item: Learn something immediately — related to what surprised you
activities.why: Surprise primes brain for learning; information absorbed now is better retained (elevated dopamine enhances memory consolidation).

activities.item: Join an improv or theater workshop
activities.why: Improv directly trains responsiveness to the unexpected — the core cognitive challenge of a surprised state.
Surprised brain has loosened cognitive constraints — optimal time for radical ideation and creative breakthrough.

social.item: Surprise someone else
social.why: Prosocial extension of surprise state creates connection and shared joy — altruism amplifies positive arousal.

personal_development.item: Question something you have long believed to be true
personal_development.why: Surprise is the emotion that most naturally facilitates belief revision. Ask: 'What if I am wrong about X?'

activities.why: Intense surprise disrupts breathing; conscious breath re-integrates the experience somatically.

activities.item: Take a photo or record it — now
activities.why: Intense surprise creates the strongest episodic memories; documenting anchors them permanently.

social.why: Intense surprise benefits from social sensemaking — shared processing reduces cognitive overwhelm.

social.item: Offer to help someone
